contest.name: BattleChain Confidence Pools
contest.critical_submission_mapping: Critical is an internal search label only; submit a proven Critical candidate as CodeHawks High.

source.deployment_context: Source contest; no deployed pool address is required for paid validity. Fork tests require BattleChain RPC endpoints.

architecture.factory: UUPS-upgradeable ConfidencePoolFactory with owner-controlled registry, implementation, moderator, token allowlist, pause, and upgrade authorization.
architecture.pools: Non-upgradeable deterministic EIP-1167 ConfidencePool clones; one or many per Safe Harbor agreement.
architecture.custody: Each clone holds all stake and optional bonus ERC20 funds.
architecture.scope_commitment: Each pool stores a flat BattleChain account list that must be a subset of agreement scope and locks once registry state leaves NOT_DEPLOYED or NEW_DEPLOYMENT.
architecture.registry: Pool reads IBattleChainSafeHarborRegistry.getAttackRegistry() live and does not cache the attack registry.
resolution: SURVIVED: staker principal plus k=2 time-weighted bonus
EXPIRED: same staker payout through permissionless expiry backstop
CORRUPTED good-faith: named attacker bounty reserved for 180 days
CORRUPTED bad-faith: full pool to recoveryAddress
CORRUPTED moderator-unavailable backstop: permissionless after expiry plus 180 days when riskWindowStart was observed

trusted_roles: pool sponsor / pool owner within documented sponsor powers
untrusted_roles: named bounty attacker only for entitlement correctness
external receiver contract within the supported standard-token model
actors_and_trust.hard_rejection: Never generate, accept, validate, or promote a candidate whose exploit requires a trusted admin, sponsor, moderator, registry owner, or upgrader to be sneaky, malicious, compromised, colluding, or to intentionally misuse documented powers.
actors_and_trust.important_exception: A real unprivileged authorization bypass, privilege escalation, missing role check, or bug triggered by an ordinary honest admin action remains in scope.

registry_states.semantic_warning: UNDER_ATTACK and PROMOTION_REQUESTED mean legally attackable by whitehats, not evidence of a breach. Only CORRUPTED proves a breach.

key_parameters.pause_scope: stake and contributeBonus only; withdrawal and all settlement paths remain live

paid_scope.critical_internal: Catastrophic readily exploitable drain, insolvency, or complete irreversible failure of settlement; maps to CodeHawks High.
paid_scope.high: Direct or nearly direct funds risk or severe core-function failure, weighted by likelihood.
paid_scope.medium: Indirect or conditional funds risk or material protocol functionality/availability disruption.
paid_scope.low: Concrete incorrect function or state behavior with minimal impact and no direct funds risk.

known_intentional_behavior: EXPIRED resolution while an agreement remains in an active-risk state is correct.
Deposits during UNDER_ATTACK are deliberately allowed and receive near-zero late-entry bonus weight.
Moderator re-flag correction closes on first value-moving claim or sweep.
CORRUPTED moderator judgement is decoupled from local risk-window observation.
The no-risk-window post-expiry CORRUPTED race is accepted; moderator must flag promptly.
The 180-day automatic CORRUPTED backstop is deliberately scope-blind and staker-pessimistic.
First-observed terminal timing and the globalScore-zero fallback are accepted residual behavior.
Pool scope is a public commitment but moderator off-chain judgement decides whether corruption was in that pool scope.
SafeHarborRegistry is a trusted singleton and malicious repointing is outside the adversarial model.
CORRUPTED bounty/sweep functions are repeat-callable to recover later donations.
proof_requirements: Pin the exact scoped file, function, and commit.
Use an unprivileged attacker unless proving a genuine role bypass.
Provide a reachable transaction and registry-state sequence.
Identify the violated accounting, lifecycle, scope, finality, or availability invariant.
Classify impact and likelihood under CodeHawks High/Medium/Low rules.
Check docs/DESIGN.md and reject documented accepted behavior before promotion.
Prefer a coded Foundry PoC; do not treat DeepWiki output as proof.
